title: ALL CAPS: THE HISTORY OF THE AMERICAN BASEBALL HAT
chaptersLine: Forty-plus chapters drafted, plus an introduction and a closing coda.
spanLine: From before the Civil War to the drops of 2026.
thisWeek: Wove in a first-person visit to an East LA shop that has held the same corner of Whittier Boulevard since 2005.
Closed out a century-old puzzle about why two big-league clubs spell a common word with an X, now sourced and confirmed.
Ran a clean fact-check pass on who actually owns the two biggest names in caps, and corrected the record.
chapterHighlights: The Philadelphia repair shop that came back from near-death on the strength of two borrowed jerseys.
The East Los Angeles corner where a fitted cap quietly became a neighborhood flag.
The common word two teams spell with an X, and the American spelling reform that survives only on their caps.
The year the underbill quietly changed color, and why the players swore by the switch.
